title: Half-Bothan
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Lirondo.jpg]] |name=Half-Bothan |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]][[Jedi vs. Sith 4|''Jedi vs. Sith'' 4]] |class=[[Equine/Legends|Equine]] |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Pale]]''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' |haircolor=*Brown *White *Grey *Black *Blonde |feathers= |eyecolor=Red |distinctions=*Unguligrade [[Limb/Legends|legs]] *Pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]] |lifespan= |origin=[[Farfalla system/Legends|Farfalla]]{{Twitter|jasoncfry|status/416715724455809024|[[Jason Fry]]|nobackup=1}} |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|Ahhh, Lirondo, with friends like these, one need not be afraid his soul will go over to the dark side!|Valenthyne Farfalla|''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]''}} A '''half-Bothan''', [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Farfalla system/Legends|Farfalla]]. It was distinguished by [[Hair/Legends|furred]], unguligrade lower [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], long tails, pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]] and small forehead horns. Several half-[[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]], including [[Lirondo]], formed part of [[Farfalla's Knights]], a private [[Army/Legends|army]] led by [[Jedi Lord]] [[Valenthyne Farfalla]]. Farfalla's Knights fought on the side of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[New Sith Wars]], circa [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|What has kept you? Did your hooves take too long to polish? Was your tail too tangled?|Lord Hoth confronts Valenthyne Farfalla|}} There existed in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that was [[Hybrid/Legends|half]]-[[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]].''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' The hybrid species was [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], with two [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and two legs, and exhibited several [[Equine/Legends|equine]] characteristics. For instance, the species was [[ungulate]]; that is, it walked on cloven hooves, with the hock joint high up the leg. The legs were thickly [[Hair/Legends|furred]] down to the knee, and a long, hair tail grew from the posterior to almost to the ground. Half-Bothans pranced, and could run sure-footed in even dim light. [[File:Half-Bothans_disembark.jpg|thumb|left|Half-Bothans displayed ungulate traits, as well as long tails.]] The species's arms were sparsely haired and ended in dexterous hands with four fingers and an opposable thumb, each tipped with a blunt nail. The head of the species had two small, red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]—from which they could emit [[Water/Legends|tears]]—a small [[Nose/Legends|nose]] with two nostrils, and a mouth with white, blunt [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. The face was plastic and largely hairless, although some [[Gender/Legends|males]] of the species grew long side burns, and stubble or a goatee on the chin. The species had pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]], and some members of the species grew two twisted, pointed horns from the top of their [[Bone/Legends|craniums]]. Members of the species grew hair on the top of the head around the horns. On some individuals, hair was a consistent [[Color/Legends|color]] across the body, while on other individuals the tail and head hair were one color, such as blonde, while the leg fur was a second color, such as brown. Other individuals exhibited black or grey hair. The species had pale skin pigmentation. The species had two sexes: male and female, with females distinguished by [[Breast/Legends|breasts]]. At least some members of the species had [[Force-sensitive/Legends|the capacity]] to use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. ==Society and culture== [[File:Farfalla-JvS6.jpg|thumb|right|Valenthyne Farfalla was part of a noble lineage of half-Bothans.]] {{Quote|Hoth and him could never see eye to eye… Hoth is old school Jedi, and Farfalla is… Farfalla. He respects Hoth but that equine blood of his is–''"
"''Trouble?|Kiel Charney and [[Unidentified red-haired Jedi|another Jedi]] discuss intra-Jedi dynamics|}} During the period of the [[New Sith Wars]], the half-Bothan species had developed a [[King/Legends|royal]] lineage in the Farfalla [[Blood/Legends|bloodline]]''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]'' as exemplified by Lord [[Valenthyne Farfalla's father|Farfalla]] and his son [[Valenthyne Farfalla]]. The royal Farfalla family had distinctive colors—variations of yellow and red—and wore elaborate attire. In combat, this included shoulder armor and [[Gold/Legends|golden]] breastplates that were buffed, polished, and adorned with [[Ruby/Legends|rubies]] and filigree; quilted red vests trimmed with bright red, puffed, [[Silk/Legends|silk]] sleeves; embroidered [[loincloth]]s; and long [[Cape/Legends|capes]] and [[Robe/Legends|robes]]. Female half-Bothans wore elaborate headgear. Half-Bothan accessories included circlets, jewelled [[Bracelet/Legends|bracelets]], and [[Piercing/Legends|earrings]]. Half-Bothans of this era wore their head hair long, often in ponytails, and their tails were often bound in cloth straps. In combat, half-Bothans wore body armor, gloves, and protection on the upper thighs composed of curved panels hung together by chain. Weaponry of choice included [[Axe/Legends|axes]], [[Pike/Legends|pikes]] and [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]]; the latter reserved for those [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] in the ways of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Some half-Bothans were considered temperamental or volatile, a characteristic that was attributed by some to their equine heritage. Valenthyne Farfalla was considered vain by some, with a reputation for giving exaggerated attention to his personal appearance,''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' including manicured nails and the use of [[Perfume/Legends|perfume]]. Others considered him more favorably, as a true and loyal [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. When confronted with the [[Death/Legends|death]] of a peer, Farfalla promised to fast for the deceased, placing two fingers on the forehead of the deceased. Half-Bothans could speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]]. ==History== {{Quote|As an envoy, you talked my Lord Father into joining the ranks of the Republic...|Valenthyne Farfalla meditates on the corpse of Pernicar|}} A population of half-Bothans developed by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the New Sith Wars, fought between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[New Sith]] between [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]] and [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]. The half-Bothans developed a society on Farfalla, a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Indra-su-Mar sector/Legends|Indra-su-Mar sector]],{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} led by the eponymous Lord Farfalla. The community was not part of the Republic until [[Pernicar]], a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and envoy, persuaded Lord Farfalla to enter the Republic. Valenthyne Farfalla, the Lord's son, became a renowned [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. He led a force of 300 [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] known as "[[Farfalla's Knights]]," many of whom were members of the same species, including his lieutenant, [[Lirondo]].[[Jedi vs. Sith 5|''Jedi vs. Sith'' 5]] ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Satyr_bounty_hunter_profile.jpg|thumb|right|upright|A character from Macan's ''Vader's Quest'' resembles a half–Bothan.]] {{Quote|Calling Farfalla 'half-Bothan' was another of those little tricks. There's just a whisper of a hint there that his people might be the product of Sith breeding experiments, and I'm simultaneously nodding, without defining anything, to Leland Chee's statement that all humanoids with pointy ears are probably descended from the Sephi.|Paul Urquhart|}} [[Darko Macan]] created this species along with the character Valenthyne Farfalla for ''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]'', a [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] in [[2001]]. The species was pencilled by [[Ramón F. Bachs]] and colored by [[Raul Fernandez]], and its design evokes the [[Wikipedia:satyr|satyrs]]{{Blog|DrewK|2|Why I ruined everything for everyone: Continuity in Path of Destruction}} or [[Wikipedia:faun|fauns]] of mythology.{{Suvudu|subdomain=star-wars|url=2012/04/crazy-plans-and-lucky-shots-writing-star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare.html|text=Crazy Plans and Lucky Shots — Writing Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare}} The comic book includes several background characters that also had horned foreheads but were [[Wikipedia:plantigrade|plantigrade]] instead of ungulate. This article does not cover those individuals, considering them a separate species. In one panel, Farfalla is depicted with blue eyes, while in all others, they are red. This article treats the blue eyes as an inconsistency. One of Macan's earlier works, the comic book [[Star Wars: Vader's Quest|''Vader's Quest'']] (published in [[1999]]), includes a [[Unidentified green bounty hunter|background character]] who is similar in appearance to this species, albeit with green skin and a bald head.[[Vader's Quest 1|''Vader's Quest'' 1]] Macan never identified Farfalla's species by name. A reference to Farfalla's temperament stemming from his "equine blood" led some fans of the series to speculate that this was the name of the species; however, when clarification was sought from [[Leland Chee]], the arbiter of the [[Holocron continuity database]], he demurred and shared his personal theory that "all pointy-eared species are either [[Sephi/Legends|Sephi]] or closely related to Sephi,"{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1395#16675997|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2007-06-08|time=6:48 AM|quote=For the longest time we've used dozens of pointy-eared characters without a species name for them. The pointed-eared species in the Jedi: Yoda comic were named Sephi. My theory is that all pointy-eared species are either Sephi or closely related to Sephi.}} a reference to a pointy-eared species from ''[[Jedi: Yoda]]'', published in [[2004]]. Farfalla's character later appeared in [[Drew Karpyshyn]]'s novel, ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'', which, ''inter alia'', retold the story of ''Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith''. Karpyshyn choose to avoid describing Farfalla's physical appearance, judging that this would undermine Farfalla's role as a foil for the character of Lord Hoth. He also adjusted the order of several events, and this article follows his chronology as the most recent depiction of these events. Karpyshyn also portrayed Farfalla as surviving Skere Kaan's thought bomb, despite his apparent death in the final panels of the comic book. This article assumes that it was other members of this species that are depicted dying during that battle. Farfalla also appeared in Karpyshyn's next novel, ''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]''. [[File:BothanSpies-SWR.png|thumb|left|An illustration of Bothans from the early period of the Expanded Universe]] Farfalla was later mentioned in several reference sources, such as ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' and [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]. [[2009]]'s ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' contained a new illustration of Farfalla by [[Chris Trevas]] that replicated the satyr-like appearance from ''Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith''. Further illustrations of Farfalla by [[Chris Scalf]] were included in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', published in [[2012]]. This reference work, written by [[Jason Fry]] with [[Paul R. Urquhart|Paul Urquhart]], also specifies that Farfalla was a half-Bothan. Urquhart revealed that the choice of Bothan was both because they were, in his opinion, the most prominent near-Human equine species, and they had been inconsistently portrayed over the years.{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/latestnews/story/Jason_Fry_Facebook_QA_Recap_144784.asp|text=Jason Fry Facebook Q&A Recap|date=2012-04-11|archivedate=20190829210801}} For instance, in the early days of [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]], sources such as [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] depicted Bothans as more Human-like, with long hair and ears,[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] while the later Legends works settled on a design with a longer muzzle.''[[Specter of the Past]]'' (See [[:File:BorskFeylya2.jpg|here]]) Urquhart also deliberately left the remainder of the heritage of Farfalla's species unspecified; simultaneously a nod to Chee's above-mentioned Sephi theory and what he described as "a whisper of a hint" that the species might be the product of Sith breeding experiments. {{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/21728447315/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-4|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 4}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of half-Bothans}} *''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' {{Ab|Darth Bane: Path of Destruction (audiobook)}} *[[Jedi vs. Sith 4|''Jedi vs. Sith'' 4]] {{1st}} *[[Jedi vs. Sith 5|''Jedi vs. Sith'' 5]] *[[Jedi vs. Sith 6|''Jedi vs. Sith'' 6]] *''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]'' {{Ab|Darth Bane: Rule of Two (audiobook)}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *{{CSWECite|I||Farfalla, Lord Valenthyne}} *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' {{1stID|half-Bothan}} *{{InsiderCite|133|The Art of Warfare|The Art of ''Warfare''|reprint=1}} {{Po}} *{{SW|url=news/drawing-from-the-imagination-mythological-creatures-in-star-wars-part-2|text=Drawing from the Imagination: Mythological Creatures in ''Star Wars'', Part 2|int=Back from the Drawing Board|nolive=1}} *[[Star Wars: Force and Destiny Beginner Game|''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Beginner Game]] {{Po}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/21728447315/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-4|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 4}} {{Interlang |es=Equino (humanoide) }} [[Category:Expansion Region sentient species]] [[Category:Hybrid sentient species]] [[Category:Ungulate sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Half-Bothans]] }}
